author: @3dmaker699
text:
Rob, I made some graphiticnitride yesterday.  I used your video on how to make it but instead of using a kiln I used my gold metal heating machine as it has a graphite crucible and figured when the urea pallets heated up in the graphite crucible graphite would mix with the urea instead of sugar.  

I moved it all outside weighed out my urea as your video set the machine to 550 c  nice 40-knot wind blowing outside so a good day for this job. Well............then the Ammonia gas started and boy did it sink the whole block of flats out! I was horrified just how much Ammonia gas came out. Even in a 40-knot wind, the ammonia was thick on the ground. My poor neighbors. I was worried someone would call the fire brigade or the police and I couldn't walk into my flat for 3 hours.

It worked I did get some graphiticniride out of it at the end of the day, The next morning I accounted for all my neighbors and they asked me if I could smell that horrible smell yesterday afternoon and I said yes it must have been coming from that industrial plant up the road.  

God if I ever make that stuff again I will rig up some way to dissolve the gas in water.

replies.author: @ThinkingandTinkering
replies.text: lol - I am laughing with you mate - it does give off a fair bit of ammonia that's true - but no worse than a chicken farm - in fact chicken farms here have to have ammonia scrubbers on their vent systems - I think wet scrubbing is used - it's basically an activated carbon tower with a water spray at the top

replies.author: @3dmaker699
replies.text: @ThinkingandTinkering Cool I'll rig one up next time.

author: @hanslepoeter5167
text:
That's great. With this cones it also will be a lot louder. Don't know exactly why.
I built a crystal radio when I was 8 or so, i disasembled the piezo ear piece and put a cone on that. I could easily hear the BBC across the room. Remember thats in the Netherlands and it was a crystal radio only depending on the power which is in the air. I remember well  .. first the tune and then "This is BBC news". The amount of power which they transmitted must have been enormous

replies.author: @ThinkingandTinkering
replies.text: it does focus the sound that's for sure

author: @MAGnetICus_Attractus
text: Ever have one of those days the ad plays 4 seconds then freezes, so you read all the comments while you wait for that last second of ad plays then write a comment? This is one of those days. So I am going to smoke a cigarette and if it doesn't finish playing the ad I will go find something else to do.

replies.author: @ThinkingandTinkering
replies.text: lol - I can't say that I have mate - but I get it lol

replies.author: @MAGnetICus_Attractus
replies.text: @ThinkingandTinkering I reloaded three times before I got to watch. Haven't owned a car in years and I need car insurance why? The garbage youtube tries to sell me. Nice build by the way. Reminded me of the "old man headphones" I made years ago. Put 2 ear buds on the end of funnels . Granted the one picture I took was at my neighbors and there was a poster of Yoda on the wall. Red funnel for the Right ear and Lime color for the Left ear.

author: @scotttovey
text:
The sound reminds me of the audio from an old gramophone I heard in a movie years ago.
What a perfect match of steam punk.

Then again, the audio was probably worse than I remember.

You'll not likely get any complaints because the design makes it look like they are old and therefore should sound that way.

Now I'm curious what it would sound like if you hooked up a real gramophone speaker to your computer.

Some things we will never know, unless someone just happens to have an old gramophone speaker.

replies.author: @ThinkingandTinkering
replies.text: to be honest mate this is a bit of form over function that's for sure lol

author: @scotttovey
text:
"If you get a octagon it's more or less a circle."
What happened Rob, the Mrs take pizza off the menu for a while? 🤨😂😎

replies.author: @ThinkingandTinkering
replies.text: lol - if a polygon has n sides of equal length then as n tends to infinity the polygon tends to a circle - the rest is a line in the sand - so yeah maybe 8 was a bit low lol - cheers mate

author: @Arachnoid_of_the_underverse
text: Looking  excellent and certainly steam punk ,which got me thinking about the connections to the speakers. Maybe if a small cut were made in the top of the loop with a small insulator inserted to keep them separated but still rigid, its possible the arms could be used to connect the speaker wires with bare copper wire and the cables to the pc taken from under the base.
